### Post by wilee-nilee on 2010-11-30
```
#
# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE
#
# It is automatically generated by grub-mkconfig using templates
# from /etc/grub.d and settings from /etc/default/grub
#

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/00_header ###
if [ -s $prefix/grubenv ]; then
  set have_grubenv=true
  load_env
fi
set default="0"
if [ "${prev_saved_entry}" ]; then
  set saved_entry="${prev_saved_entry}"
  save_env saved_entry
  set prev_saved_entry=
  save_env prev_saved_entry
  set boot_once=true
fi

function savedefault {
  if [ -z "${boot_once}" ]; then
    saved_entry="${chosen}"
    save_env saved_entry
  fi
}

function recordfail {
  set recordfail=1
  if [ -n "${have_grubenv}" ]; then if [ -z "${boot_once}" ]; then save_env recordfail; fi; fi
}

function load_video {
  insmod vbe
}

insmod part_msdos
insmod ext2
set root='(hd0,msdos5)'
search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 857d5af9-23cd-4d9b-908b-cc075e866758
if loadfont /usr/share/grub/unicode.pf2 ; then
  set gfxmode=640x480
  load_video
  insmod gfxterm
fi
terminal_output gfxterm
insmod part_msdos
insmod ext2
set root='(hd0,msdos5)'
search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 857d5af9-23cd-4d9b-908b-cc075e866758
set locale_dir=($root)/boot/grub/locale
set lang=en
insmod gettext
if [ "${recordfail}" = 1 ]; then
  set timeout=-1
else
  set timeout=5
fi
### END /etc/grub.d/00_header ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/05_debian_theme ###
set menu_color_normal=white/black
set menu_color_highlight=black/light-gray
### END /etc/grub.d/05_debian_theme ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/10_linux ###
menuentry 'Ubuntu, with Linux 2.6.35-23-generic' --class ubuntu --class gnu-linux --class gnu --class os {
	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos5)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 857d5af9-23cd-4d9b-908b-cc075e866758
	linux	/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.35-23-generic root=UUID=857d5af9-23cd-4d9b-908b-cc075e866758 ro   ipv6.disable=1 quiet splash
	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-23-generic
}
menuentry 'Ubuntu, with Linux 2.6.35-23-generic (recovery mode)' --class ubuntu --class gnu-linux --class gnu --class os {
	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos5)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 857d5af9-23cd-4d9b-908b-cc075e866758
	echo	'Loading Linux 2.6.35-23-generic ...'
	linux	/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.35-23-generic root=UUID=857d5af9-23cd-4d9b-908b-cc075e866758 ro single 
	echo	'Loading initial ramdisk ...'
	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-23-generic
}
### END /etc/grub.d/10_linux ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/20_linux_xen ###
### END /etc/grub.d/20_linux_xen ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/30_os-prober ###
menuentry "Windows 7 (loader) (on /dev/sda1)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ntfs
	set root='(hd0,msdos1)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 6a187e5e62ffeaf6
	chainloader +1
}
### END /etc/grub.d/30_os-prober ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/40_custom ###
# This file provides an easy way to add custom menu entries.  Simply type the
# menu entries you want to add after this comment.  Be careful not to change
# the 'exec tail' line above.
### END /etc/grub.d/40_custom ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/41_custom ###
if [ -f  $prefix/custom.cfg ]; then
  source $prefix/custom.cfg;
fi
### END /etc/grub.d/41_custom ###
```

Sorry no XP it is in a virtual where it belongs but W7 is in there.

   search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set bdb3d2a39d0b85dd

### Post by oldfred on 2010-12-01
I have a bunch that I have saved, but you have to use your UUID:
To get your UUID
sudo blkid 

```
/etc/grub.d/40_custom
menuentry "Windows NT/2000/XP (loader) (on /dev/sdb5)" {
    insmod ntfs
    set root=(hd1,5)
    search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 4a6077fc6077ed57
    drivemap -s (hd1) ${root}
    chainloader +1
}

# This method applies to Windows and to FreeDOS
menuentry “Windows XP on sdb1, by chainloader”  {
drivemap -s (hd0) (hd1)
set root=(hd0)
chainloader  (hd1,1)+1
 }

menuentry "Windows XP on sda1" {
       insmod ntfs
       set root=(hd0,1)
       search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set a2f48f73f48f490d
       drivemap -s (hd0) ${root}
       chainloader +1
}

# Chainload another bootloader
menuentry "Chainload partition 3" {
set root=(hd0,3)
chainloader +1
}

```


Windows has to have its code in the windows boot sector and the boot.ini has to refer to the correct drive & partition.

---
